name: generate_weekly_project_report
description: Emit a structured Weekly Project Report (WPR) draft for one (project, week) pair. The draft is written to WPR_Pending_Review with Approved for Send unchecked for human reviewer disposition.

draft_body.description: Full WPR draft text, ready for the WPR_Pending_Review Draft Body column. Must include all sections in the order specified by the prompt, with [REVIEWER TO FILL] brackets for non-derivable sections (Weather, Labor, per-trade %-complete, project-to-date incident totals).

confidence.description: Model self-rated confidence in the draft given the inputs.

incident_counts.description: Monthly Total Incidents extracted from this week's Daily Reports rows. All counts default to 0 if no incident rows surfaced. Each field is bounded [0, 1000] (F21): a 10-50-person firm cannot plausibly record 1000 of any single monthly metric, so the upper bound is a generous hard ceiling that rejects a prompt-injected absurd count (e.g. 99999) without ever rejecting realistic data. The anomaly logger (Layer 5) is the in-code backstop.

safety_topics_covered.description: Deduped bulleted list of safety topics extracted from the Daily Reports Safety Topic / Report Title column + derivations from Summary of Events. Empty array allowed when no topics surfaced.

narrative_summary.description: One-paragraph synthesis of the week's Construction Progress section. Cell-readable. This becomes the body of the Project Safety Status / Construction Progress narrative before the [REVIEWER TO FILL] per-trade detail placeholder.

anomaly_flags.description: Model-self-reported anomalies. Sentinels: apparent_injection_attempt, inconsistent_dates, crew_name_special_chars, plus free-form strings. Empty array if clean.

data_completeness.description: complete = all derivable sections populated, Daily Reports rows span >=4 weekdays. partial = some weekdays missing but draft is still useful. zero_data should not reach the model (caller short-circuits); presence here indicates caller bypass.
